summary.text: [0-4] A static title card features white text reading "How to paint a vibrant turquoise background" centered on a solid black background. [4-6] The video cuts to a horizontal rectangular canvas displaying a smooth gradient, glowing bright turquoise in the center and fading into dark teal at the corners, captioned "like this one". [6-14] The scene shifts to a blank white canvas with overlays "By Amy Pearce" and "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com". A hand enters holding a grey bottle, then two hands present paint tubes. The left hand lifts a black tube showing "WINSOR & NEWTON" and "33", while the right hand holds a white tube over the surface.

[14-18] Two hands present paint tubes over a white canvas beneath static text "By Amy Pearce" and a URL. The left hand holds a dark tube labeled "WINTON 35," while the right hand displays a white tube, rotating it to reveal the number "40." [18-22] A third tube with a green label reading "BOSS OIL COLOR" is introduced. The right hand gathers the dark, white, and green tubes into a single cluster. [22-28] A fourth tube, a crumpled blue "WINTON 33" with white paint residue near the cap, is held in the left hand. The right hand maintains a grip on the cluster of three tubes centrally above the canvas. The text overlays persist at the top and bottom edges throughout the sequence.

[28-32] A green tube labeled "BOSS" moves to the left hand, joining a crumpled blue tube, while the right hand holds a dark tube marked "35" and a white tube marked "40". Text overlays "By Amy Pearce" and a blog URL persist. [32-36] The hands exit, leaving the white canvas empty, before a hand enters with a large clear plastic bottle featuring a yellow label, moving it rapidly across the frame. [36-40] The top text changes to "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and the bottom to "herARTfromtheATTIC" as the bottle is set aside. A hand then presents a wide paintbrush with dark bristles centrally over the canvas. [40-42] A second hand introduces a clump of brown, fibrous material, resembling steel wool, holding it directly above the brush bristles while the text overlays remain fixed.

[42-46] A hand touches a brown fibrous clump to the dark bristles of a wide paintbrush held over a white canvas, then clears the tools from view. [46-52] Two hands hold a dark paint tube with a white cap centrally, twisting to unscrew it before the left hand departs. [52-56] The right hand moves the tube to the canvas's right edge, pressing it down to dispense a small black mark. Throughout, text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" remain fixed at the top and bottom borders.

Between 58-64s, a hand squeezes a dark tube to deposit black paint dots and smears along the bottom edge of a white canvas, extending the line leftward to the corner. At 64s, the artist switches to a tube with a green label. Holding it with both hands initially, they position it on the left side of the canvas. From 68-70s, they squeeze a streak of greenish paint onto the surface near the existing black marks on the left edge. Throughout the sequence, static text overlays reading "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" remain fixed at the top and bottom borders.

[70-74] A hand squeezes a dark greenish streak on the left canvas side, then adds small black dots along the bottom right and right edge. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the borders. [74-80] Using a dark paint tube, the hand deposits black blobs: one center-left above the green streak, another near the right edge vertical line, and a third near existing center-left marks. [80-82] A hand holding a white paint tube enters from the bottom right, moving toward the canvas center above the scattered paint marks.

[82-88] A hand holding a white paint tube applies small dabs of white paint across the center and right side of a white canvas, adding to existing black and green marks along the perimeter. Text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" remain fixed at the borders. [88-92] The hand switches to holding a clear plastic bottle with a yellow label horizontally, manipulating the cap and then tilting the bottle downwards. [92-94] A small white bowl featuring a floral rim pattern is introduced above the canvas center, held while the bottle remains visible below.

From 94-100s, a hand tilts a clear plastic bottle with a yellow label, pouring transparent liquid into a small white bowl with a floral rim resting on a white canvas scattered with black and green paint marks. Text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the borders. Between 100-104s, the hand shifts the bottle from the bowl to the top left edge of the canvas, placing it down. Finally, at 104-106s, a hand holding a wide paintbrush with dark bristles dips the brush into the liquid-filled floral bowl.

[110-118] A hand holding a wide brush with dark bristles moves to the right edge of a white canvas, applying vertical downward strokes to spread material over existing black paint marks along the border. While a floral bowl is initially noted as stationary near the center as the painting action commences, it is described as absent from the surface by 114s. Throughout the sequence, static text overlays remain fixed at the frame boundaries: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. The hand maintains these vertical brushing motions, pushing the dark paint mixture further down the side and extending coverage towards the bottom corner. The wide bristles spread the substance over the black marks continuously.

[108-126] A hand lifts a wide brush with dark bristles from a floral bowl containing clear liquid near the center of a white canvas, while another hand withdraws. The brush moves to the right edge, hovering over scattered black paint marks. The hand maneuvers the brush vertically along the right border, spreading a dark mixture over existing black marks with up and down strokes. The coverage extends downwards along the side border towards the bottom corner. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" (top) and "herARTfromtheATTIC" (bottom) remain fixed throughout the painting action.

[126-138] A hand holding a wide brush applies dark paint along the right vertical edge and top right corner of a white surface. The brush moves upwards and across, spreading the dark substance to create a border. A small floral bowl sits centrally on the white area amidst scattered black marks. Static text overlays remain fixed: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. The action focuses on coating the rim of the surface with the dark paint.

[138-148] A hand holding a wide brush applies dark paint along the left edge of the white canvas, extending a dark border. The brush spreads the dark substance along the rim. A floral bowl remains stationary in the center amidst scattered black marks. Static text overlays persist at the top and bottom boundaries: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC".

[148-158] A hand holding a wide brush applies dark paint to extend a border around a central white area containing a stationary floral bowl. The brush moves downwards along the left edge, spreading the dark substance vertically. It then transitions to the bottom edge, moving left to right over the white surface. Finally, the hand maneuvers the brush vertically upwards along the right edge, blending the new paint with the existing dark area. Throughout, the floral bowl remains fixed amidst scattered marks, and static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the boundaries.

Between 158s and 166s, a hand maneuvers a wide brush along the upper right edge and top section of the canvas, applying dark paint to extend the black border. The brush moves horizontally leftwards, covering the white surface near the top corner and blending with the existing black area to connect the borders. This action further encloses the central region where a floral bowl remains stationary. Static text overlays reading "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom persist at the frame boundaries throughout the painting process.

From 168s to 176s, a hand maneuvers a wide brush along the top edge of the canvas, applying dark paint to expand the black border from right to left. The brush strokes cover the white surface, further enclosing the central area where a floral bowl remains stationary. Static text overlays 'DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work' and 'herARTfromtheATTIC' persist at the frame boundaries. Towards the end, a left hand holds a white bottle over the top left corner while the right hand holds the brush, as the black border surrounds the central white area containing the floral bowl.

[176-184] A hand pours clear liquid from a white plastic bottle onto the bristles of a wide brush and then directly onto the central white canvas surface near the right edge. As the liquid spreads on the surface, the hand moves the bottle left towards a floral bowl with a patterned rim and sets it down. Another hand holds the wide brush with dark bristles, eventually using it to apply dark paint to the bottom left corner, extending the black border surrounding the wet white area. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom persist throughout the sequence.

Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the boundaries. A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with dark paint along the left edge near a floral bowl, extending the black border onto the wet white surface [184-186]. The brush moves into the central white area, applying a dark pigment stroke [186-188]. Then, the hand moves the brush vertically upwards, depositing thick dark teal paint to create a broad stroke covering the white surface from bottom to top [188-190]. Finally, the brush spreads the dark teal pigment upwards and slightly right across the central area, contrasting with the black border on the left [190-192].

[192-196] A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the central white area of a canvas. The brush strokes spread the pigment from the left side towards the right, covering the white surface with a broad layer of color while the black border remains visible on the periphery. The hand sweeps the brush to extend the colored area further right, blending the pigment into the white surface. [196-200] Subsequently, the hand guides the brush downwards and rightwards across the canvas. These strokes extend the colored area further towards the bottom right, covering more of the white surface near the bottom right of the central patch. The black border remains distinct along the edges as the teal layer expands outward. The text "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" appears at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[200-208] A hand guides a wide brush loaded with teal paint across a canvas featuring a black border on the left. Initially, the brush spreads pigment rightwards along the bottom edge, covering the white surface near the lower right. The hand then maneuvers the brush upwards, depositing pigment onto the white canvas and extending the colored area vertically. The brush strokes blend the teal color into the white surface, moving upwards and rightwards to expand the teal layer over the central area. The black border remains visible on the periphery, framing the expanding region. Text overlays display "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[208-216] A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the canvas. Initially, the brush spreads pigment rightwards and downwards, blending it into the white surface while a black border remains visible on the left. The motion shifts to sweeping upwards, expanding the teal area vertically. Subsequently, the hand guides the brush leftwards and downwards, spreading pigment over the dark surface on the left and blending it into the black background. The teal coverage expands over the white surface and into the black background. Static text overlays persist throughout: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[216-224] A hand holds a textured yellow sponge over a canvas displaying radiating teal brushstrokes on a black background. The sponge is initially positioned near the center before moving to the right side of the painted area. The hand presses the sponge against the wet paint, dabbing gently from right to center to blend the pigment and soften the texture of the strokes. Static white text overlays read "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. A floral plate sits on the left periphery.

[224-232] A hand presses a textured yellow sponge onto wet teal paint on a black canvas, dabbing gently to blend radiating brushstrokes. The sponge moves across the central area to soften the pigment's texture, then shifts slightly right. It returns to the center before moving from the center-right downwards towards the bottom left edge of the teal shape, blending the paint into the dark surface. Static white text overlays remain fixed: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. The black background surrounding the teal shape is unchanged.

[232-240] A hand holding a textured yellow sponge applies pressure to a canvas, dabbing teal paint to blend it into a black background. The action begins at the top left corner, where the sponge mixes the pigment near the upper edge. The hand then repositions to the bottom right corner and lower edge, continuing to dab and soften the transition between the teal and dark areas. Throughout the sequence, static white text overlays remain fixed: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

From 240s to 248s, a hand holding a textured yellow sponge works on the top right corner of a canvas. The sponge dabs the teal paint, blending the pigment into the black background near the upper edge to soften the transition between colors. The hand presses against the surface, continuing this dabbing motion along the edge to ensure the paint integrates smoothly. Throughout the sequence, static white text overlays remain fixed at the frame boundaries: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" appears at the top, while "herARTfromtheATTIC" is displayed at the bottom.

From 248s to 256s, a hand holding a textured yellow sponge repeatedly dabs the top right corner and upper edge of a canvas. The sponge presses against wet teal paint, blending the pigment into the surrounding black background to soften the color transition. Throughout this process, static white text overlays remain fixed on the screen: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[256-264] A hand holding a blue sponge dabs the canvas, blending teal paint into a black background. Initially, the sponge presses against the wet surface near the upper edge and top right corner to soften the transition. The hand moves the sponge across the surface, softening the texture of the pigment. Later, the sponge dabs the bottom right and top left corners to blend the edges. Static white text overlays reading 'DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work' and 'herARTfromtheATTIC' remain fixed at the top and bottom.

[264-268] A hand holding a blue sponge dabs the top right corner of a canvas, blending teal paint into the black background before exiting the frame. [268-270] The scene remains static with no visible movement, displaying the gradient artwork. [270-272] A hand lifts the left edge of the canvas, tilting it upward to reveal a paint-splattered table surface underneath featuring dried orange, yellow, and black marks. The canvas is maneuvered by hands, exposing its side profile. Static white text overlays persist at the frame boundaries throughout: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[272-274] Hands lift a teal and black gradient canvas, tilting it upward to reveal a paint-splattered table surface underneath featuring dried orange, yellow, and black marks. [274-276] The rectangular canvas lies flat and centered, with a background revealing a white stove with black knobs on the right and orange shelving above. Static white text overlays reading "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the top and bottom boundaries. [276-278] The camera zooms in significantly on the canvas, filling the majority of the frame with the teal and black gradient surface and obscuring the surrounding environment. [278-280] The camera zooms out to show the full rectangular canvas lying flat, making the teal and black gradient completely visible again alongside the white stove and shelving, while static text overlays remain.

[280-284] The camera zooms in on a teal and black gradient canvas, filling the frame with the painted surface and cropping out kitchen elements. The bright turquoise center dominates while dark edges fade. [284-286] The angle shifts left, revealing the canvas edge and paint-splattered surface beneath. [286-288] The camera zooms out to show the entire rectangular canvas on a messy table. Background elements like orange shelving and a white stove reappear. Static white text overlays persist: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[288-290] A rectangular canvas with a teal gradient, bright center to dark edges, lies on a paint-splattered table. White text overlays read "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. Background shows orange shelving and a white stove.
[290-294] The text overlays disappear, leaving the teal gradient canvas flat on the messy surface. The camera holds a steady shot of the artwork with background details visible on the periphery.
[294-296] The scene cuts to a black background. Centered white text appears reading "There's pleanty more where this came from!" across three lines. This title card replaces the studio view, signaling the end of the clip with a promotional message.

[296-298] The video cuts to a solid black background with centered white text reading "There's pleanty more where this came from!" across three lines. [298-300] This transitions to a final screen featuring the text "SUBSCRIBE! For more art tutorials like this one!" at the top and the URL "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com" at the bottom. The center displays a painting of a dark, twisting branch with white flowers against a teal background. [300-302] The central artwork changes to a triptych of three square panels featuring glowing white dandelions with green stems against a teal background. [302-304] The artwork switches to a four-panel composition displaying white flowers and dark branches against a teal backdrop, while the white text overlays at the top and bottom remain static.

From 68-70s, they squeeze a streak of greenish paint onto the surface near the existing black marks on the left edge. Throughout the sequence, static text overlays reading "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" remain fixed at the top and bottom borders.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card features white text reading "How to paint a vibrant turquoise background" centered on a solid black background. [4-6] The video cuts to a horizontal rectangular canvas displaying a smooth gradient, glowing bright turquoise in the center and fading into dark teal at the corners, captioned "like this one". [6-14] The scene shifts to a blank white canvas with overlays "By Amy Pearce" and "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com". A hand enters holding a grey bottle, then two hands present paint tubes. The left hand lifts a black tube showing "WINSOR & NEWTON" and "33", while the right hand holds a white tube over the surface.

[14-18] Two hands present paint tubes over a white canvas beneath static text "By Amy Pearce" and a URL. The left hand holds a dark tube labeled "WINTON 35," while the right hand displays a white tube, rotating it to reveal the number "40." [18-22] A third tube with a green label reading "BOSS OIL COLOR" is introduced. The right hand gathers the dark, white, and green tubes into a single cluster. [22-28] A fourth tube, a crumpled blue "WINTON 33" with white paint residue near the cap, is held in the left hand. The right hand maintains a grip on the cluster of three tubes centrally above the canvas. The text overlays persist at the top and bottom edges throughout the sequence.

[28-32] A green tube labeled "BOSS" moves to the left hand, joining a crumpled blue tube, while the right hand holds a dark tube marked "35" and a white tube marked "40". [32-36] The hands exit, leaving the white canvas empty, before a hand enters with a large clear plastic bottle featuring a yellow label, moving it rapidly across the frame. [36-40] The top text changes to "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and the bottom to "herARTfromtheATTIC" as the bottle is set aside. A hand then presents a wide paintbrush with dark bristles centrally over the canvas. [42-46] A hand touches a brown fibrous clump to the dark bristles of a wide paintbrush held over a white canvas, then clears the tools from view. [46-52] Two hands hold a dark paint tube with a white cap centrally, twisting to unscrew it before the left hand departs. [52-56] The right hand moves the tube to the canvas's right edge, pressing it down to dispense a small black mark. compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card features white text reading "How to paint a vibrant turquoise background" centered on a solid black background. [4-6] The video cuts to a horizontal rectangular canvas displaying a smooth gradient, glowing bright turquoise in the center and fading into dark teal at the corners, captioned "like this one". [6-14] The scene shifts to a blank white canvas with overlays "By Amy Pearce" and "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com". A hand enters holding a grey bottle, then two hands present paint tubes. The left hand lifts a black tube showing "WINSOR & NEWTON" and "33", while the right hand holds a white tube over the surface. [14-18] Two hands present paint tubes over a white canvas beneath static text "By Amy Pearce" and a URL. The left hand holds a dark tube labeled "WINTON 35," while the right hand displays a white tube, rotating it to reveal the number "40." [18-22] A third tube with a green label reading "BOSS OIL COLOR" is introduced. The right hand gathers the dark, white, and green tubes into a single cluster. [22-28] A fourth tube, a crumpled blue "WINTON 33" with white paint residue near the cap, is held in the left hand.

[70-74] A hand squeezes a dark greenish streak on the left canvas side, then adds small black dots along the bottom right and right edge. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the borders. [74-80] Using a dark paint tube, the hand deposits black blobs: one center-left above the green streak, another near the right edge vertical line, and a third near existing center-left marks. [80-82] A hand holding a white paint tube enters from the bottom right, moving toward the canvas center above the scattered paint marks.

[82-88] A hand holding a white paint tube applies small dabs of white paint across the center and right side of a white canvas, adding to existing black and green marks along the perimeter. Text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" remain fixed at the borders. [88-92] The hand switches to holding a clear plastic bottle with a yellow label horizontally, manipulating the cap and then tilting the bottle downwards. [92-94] A small white bowl featuring a floral rim pattern is introduced above the canvas center, held while the bottle remains visible below.

From 94-100s, a hand tilts a clear plastic bottle with a yellow label, pouring transparent liquid into a small white bowl with a floral rim resting on a white canvas scattered with black and green paint marks. Text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the borders. Between 100-104s, the hand shifts the bottle from the bowl to the top left edge of the canvas, placing it down. Finally, at 104-106s, a hand holding a wide paintbrush with dark bristles dips the brush into the liquid-filled floral bowl.

[110-118] A hand holding a wide brush with dark bristles moves to the right edge of a white canvas, applying vertical downward strokes to spread material over existing black paint marks along the border. While a floral bowl is initially noted as stationary near the center as the painting action commences, it is described as absent from the surface by 114s. Throughout the sequence, static text overlays remain fixed at the frame boundaries: "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom. The hand maintains these vertical brushing motions, pushing the dark paint mixture further down the side and extending coverage towards the bottom corner. The wide bristles spread the substance over the black marks continuously.

[148-158] A hand holding a wide brush applies dark paint to extend a border around a central white area containing a stationary floral bowl. A hand maneuvers a wide brush along the top edge, applying dark paint to extend the black border further across the white surface. The brush strokes cover the area near the top, continuing to enclose the central white region containing the floral bowl. Static text overlays remain fixed at the frame boundaries.

A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the central white area. The brush strokes spread the pigment from the left side towards the right, covering the white surface with a broad layer of color. The black border remains visible on the periphery as the teal paint expands.

A hand sweeps a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the canvas, extending the colored area further to the right. The strokes blend the pigment into the white surface, increasing coverage in the central region. The black border remains distinct along the edges as the teal layer expands outward.

A hand guides a wide brush loaded with teal paint downwards and rightwards across the canvas. The brush strokes extend the colored area, covering more of the white surface near the bottom right of the central patch. The black border remains visible on the periphery as the teal layer continues to expand.

A hand guides a wide brush loaded with teal paint downwards across the canvas. The brush strokes extend the colored area further towards the bottom right, covering more of the white surface. The black border remains visible on the periphery as the teal layer continues to expand.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card features white text reading "How to paint a vibrant turquoise background" centered on a solid black background. [4-6] The video cuts to a horizontal rectangular canvas displaying a smooth gradient, glowing bright turquoise in the center and fading into dark teal at the corners, captioned "like this one". [6-14] The scene shifts to a blank white canvas with overlays "By Amy Pearce" and "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com". A hand enters holding a grey bottle, then two hands present paint tubes. The left hand lifts a black tube showing "WINSOR & NEWTON" and "33", while the right hand holds a white tube over the surface. [14-18] Two hands present paint tubes over a white canvas beneath static text "By Amy Pearce" and a URL. The left hand holds a dark tube labeled "WINTON 35," while the right hand displays a white tube, rotating it to reveal the number "40." [18-22] A third tube with a green label reading "BOSS OIL COLOR" is introduced. The right hand gathers the dark, white, and green tubes into a single cluster. [22-28] A fourth tube, a crumpled blue "WINTON 33" with white paint residue near the cap, is held in the left hand.

From 168s to 176s, a hand maneuvers a wide brush along the top edge of the canvas, applying dark paint to expand the black border from right to left. The brush strokes cover the white surface, further enclosing the central area where a floral bowl remains stationary. Static text overlays 'DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work' and 'herARTfromtheATTIC' persist at the frame boundaries. Towards the end, a left hand holds a white bottle over the top left corner while the right hand holds the brush, as the black border surrounds the central white area containing the floral bowl.

[176-184] A hand pours clear liquid from a white plastic bottle onto the bristles of a wide brush and then directly onto the central white canvas surface near the right edge. As the liquid spreads on the surface, the hand moves the bottle left towards a floral bowl with a patterned rim and sets it down. Another hand holds the wide brush with dark bristles, eventually using it to apply dark paint to the bottom left corner, extending the black border surrounding the wet white area. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom persist throughout the sequence.

Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the boundaries. A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with dark paint along the left edge near a floral bowl, extending the black border onto the wet white surface [184-186]. The brush moves into the central white area, applying a dark pigment stroke [186-188]. Then, the hand moves the brush vertically upwards, depositing thick dark teal paint to create a broad stroke covering the white surface from bottom to top [188-190]. Finally, the brush spreads the dark teal pigment upwards and slightly right across the central area, contrasting with the black border on the left [190-192].

[192-196] A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the central white area of a canvas. The brush strokes spread the pigment from the left side towards the right, covering the white surface with a broad layer of color while the black border remains visible on the periphery. The hand sweeps the brush to extend the colored area further right, blending the pigment into the white surface. [196-200] Subsequently, the hand guides the brush downwards and rightwards across the canvas. These strokes extend the colored area further towards the bottom right, covering more of the white surface near the bottom right of the central patch. The black border remains distinct along the edges as the teal layer expands outward. The text "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" appears at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[6-14] The scene shifts to a blank white canvas with overlays "By Amy Pearce" and "www.herARTfromtheATTIC.blogspot.com". A hand enters holding a grey bottle, then two hands present paint tubes. The left hand lifts a black tube showing "WINSOR & NEWTON" and "33", while the right hand holds a white tube over the surface. [14-18] Two hands present paint tubes over a white canvas beneath static text "By Amy Pearce" and a URL. The left hand holds a dark tube labeled "WINTON 35," while the right hand displays a white tube, rotating it to reveal the number "40." [18-22] A third tube with a green label reading "BOSS OIL COLOR" is introduced. The right hand gathers the dark, white, and green tubes into a single cluster. [22-28] A fourth tube, a crumpled blue "WINTON 33" with white paint residue near the cap, is held in the left hand.

[176-184] A hand pours clear liquid from a white plastic bottle onto the bristles of a wide brush and then directly onto the central white canvas surface near the right edge. As the liquid spreads on the surface, the hand moves the bottle left towards a floral bowl with a patterned rim and sets it down. Another hand holds the wide brush with dark bristles, eventually using it to apply dark paint to the bottom left corner, extending the black border surrounding the wet white area. Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom persist throughout the sequence.

Static text overlays "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" and "herARTfromtheATTIC" persist at the boundaries. A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with dark paint along the left edge near a floral bowl, extending the black border onto the wet white surface [184-186]. The brush moves into the central white area, applying a dark pigment stroke [186-188]. Then, the hand moves the brush vertically upwards, depositing thick dark teal paint to create a broad stroke covering the white surface from bottom to top [188-190]. Finally, the brush spreads the dark teal pigment upwards and slightly right across the central area, contrasting with the black border on the left [190-192].

[192-196] A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the central white area of a canvas. The brush strokes spread the pigment from the left side towards the right, covering the white surface with a broad layer of color while the black border remains visible on the periphery. The hand sweeps the brush to extend the colored area further right, blending the pigment into the white surface. [196-200] Subsequently, the hand guides the brush downwards and rightwards across the canvas. These strokes extend the colored area further towards the bottom right, covering more of the white surface near the bottom right of the central patch. The black border remains distinct along the edges as the teal layer expands outward. The text "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" appears at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

[200-208] A hand guides a wide brush loaded with teal paint across a canvas featuring a black border on the left. Initially, the brush spreads pigment rightwards along the bottom edge, covering the white surface near the lower right. The hand then maneuvers the brush upwards, depositing pigment onto the white canvas and extending the colored area vertically. The brush strokes blend the teal color into the white surface, moving upwards and rightwards to expand the teal layer over the central area. The black border remains visible on the periphery, framing the expanding region. Text overlays display "DON'T FORGET to hashtag/pin your work" at the top and "herARTfromtheATTIC" at the bottom.

recent_thinks.text: A hand holds a dark paint tube vertically against the right edge of the white canvas. The tube is pressed against the surface, dispensing small dots of black paint in a vertical line along the border. Static text overlays persist at the top and bottom edges of the frame.

A hand dips a wide brush with dark bristles into a small floral bowl filled with liquid. A second hand enters to steady the bowl as the brush is swirled within the clear fluid. Scattered black paint marks surround the bowl on the white canvas surface.

A hand maneuvers a wide brush along the top edge, applying dark paint to extend the black border further across the white surface. The brush strokes cover the area near the top, continuing to enclose the central white region containing the floral bowl. Static text overlays remain fixed at the frame boundaries.

A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint across the white canvas, spreading the pigment rightwards and slightly downwards. The brush strokes blend the color into the white surface, expanding the teal layer to cover more of the central area while the black border remains visible on the left.

A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint, sweeping upwards across the canvas. The brush strokes blend the pigment into the white surface, expanding the teal area vertically while the black border remains visible on the left side. Static text overlays persist at the frame boundaries.

A hand maneuvers a wide brush loaded with teal paint, sweeping leftwards and downwards across the canvas. The brush strokes blend the pigment into the dark border area, expanding the teal coverage over the white surface. Static text overlays persist at the frame boundaries.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A static title card features white text reading "How to paint a vibrant turquoise background" centered on a solid black background. [256-264] A hand holding a blue sponge dabs the canvas, blending teal paint into a black background.
